description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular body featuring a metallic front panel and wooden side panels. The front face displays a blue-lit frequency indicator, multiple rotary knobs for tuning and volume control, and a large main dial on the right. The top surface has ventilation slots, suggesting internal electronic components that require cooling. The rear panel is equipped with various input/output ports, speaker terminals, and connection jacks, indicating compatibility with external audio equipment. The side panels are plain, showcasing the wood grain texture, and the overall build appears robust and designed for tabletop use. The combination of metal, plastic, and wood gives it a classic, high-quality appearance suitable for home or garage environments.
description: A vintage radio receiver with a metallic front panel, blue-lit display, multiple control knobs, and wooden side panels. It features rear audio connections and a robust, classic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue-lit frequency display, multiple control knobs, and a large main dial.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the plain wooden panel and the protruding control knobs at the front.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying various audio input/output ports, speaker terminals, and connection jacks.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the plain wooden panel and the protruding main dial at the front.
